title: Open to PR to fail execution if an `x` value arrives at an `if` statement?
body:
How may we help - what is your question?

The verilog standard states that an `if` statement which receives a condition of `x` should evaluate the `else` branch, as though it had received a `0` condition. This means that the following code, when simulated, will convert an `x` value into a `0` value:

```
if (foo) assign out = 1;
else assign out = 0;
```
However, when synthesized, an effective value of `foo` of `x` will actually give an effective output value of `x`. Therefore the simulation will lead to different behavior from synthesis.

What are standard solutions to this problem? I'm thinking of creating a PR that would add an option to verilator such that at runtime, if an `if` statement received an `x` value, this would cause a runtime error.

Thoughts?
